Actors: Professionals who portray fictional characters in film or television productions
Screenwriters: Professionals who write scripts for films or television.
Films: Creative works produced for cinematic distribution.
Film Genres: Categories classifying films by narrative elements and stylistic approaches.
Countries: Sovereign states recognized by international law, possessing defined territories and permanent populations.
Languages: Systems of communication used by human societies.
Historical Figures: Deceased individuals of significant impact documented in the historical record.
Professions: Occupational roles that individuals engage in for work.
Gender: A classification system categorizing individuals based on social roles or biological traits.
Person: A human individual who is a notable public figure in arts, entertainment, or other fields.
Creative Work: Artistic or intellectual productions created by humans.
Actor: Professionals who portray characters in film or television productions.
Musician: A person who composes, performs, or conducts music professionally.
Location: A specific point or area on Earth's surface.
Film: A feature-length motion picture created for theatrical exhibition.
Director: a person responsible for the creative vision and overall direction of a film or television production.
Singers: Professional artists who perform vocal music.
Occupations: Professional activities that individuals engage in for employment or creative expression.
Regions: Geographical areas defined by shared characteristics like political boundaries, climate, or culture.
Geographic Location: A defined area on Earth's surface with distinct physical or political boundaries.
Work Title: The official name given to a creative work.
Entertainment Professionals: Individuals working in film, music, or performance industries.
Sub-national Regions: Administrative divisions within a sovereign state, such as provinces, states, or counties.
Film Director: a person who directs the making of a film.
Creative Professionals: Individuals engaged in artistic, literary, or performance-based occupations.
City: A populated urban area with defined boundaries and local governance.
TV Series: A fictional narrative program produced in episodic installments for television broadcast.
County: An administrative territorial division within a state or country.
Film/TV Productions: Cinematic or televised works created for entertainment or educational purposes.
Demographic Group: Human populations sharing common characteristics studied in social sciences.
Television Show: A series of episodic video content produced for entertainment.
Drama Film: A category of cinematic works focusing on serious narratives and emotional themes.
Adventure Film: A film genre centered around exciting journeys and physical challenges.
Person (Film Producer): An individual who oversees the production of films.
Person (Songwriter): A person who composes musical compositions, typically including lyrics and melodies.
Person (Actor): A person who performs in films, television, or theater.
Person (Writer): A human who professionally creates written works.
Film Producers: Individuals who oversee the production of movies.
Legal Concept: A fundamental principle or category recognized within a legal system.
Political Figure: An individual holding or having held elected government office or significant political influence.
Describes characteristic of: Defines inherent attributes of entities like films or people.
Belongs to: Indicates membership or affiliation with a larger category or group.
has property: Describes an inherent characteristic or attribute of an entity.
Is Located In: Denotes physical containment of an entity within a geographic area.
Speaks: The languages spoken by a person or ethnic group.
Indicates localization: The adaptation of media content to specific languages.
Denotes specialization: Indicates a specific expertise area within a wider field.
Governs System Location: describes the administrative authority determining where a system is physically situated.
